title: Glycan chip based on structure switchable DNA linker for on-chip biosynthesis of cancer-associated complex glycans
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fhk6e","text":"On-chip glycan biosynthesis is an effective strategy for preparing useful complex glycan sources and for preparing glycan-involved applications simultaneously. However, current methods have some limitations when analyzing biosynthesized glycans and optimizing enzymatic reactions, which could result in undefined glycan structures on a surface, leading to unequal and unreliable results. In this work, a novel glycan chip was developed by introducing a pH-responsive i-motif DNA linker to control the immobilization and isolation of glycans on chip surfaces in a pH-dependent manner. On-chip enzymatic glycosylations were optimized for uniform biosynthesis of cancer-associated Globo H hexasaccharide and its related complex glycans through stepwise quantitative analyses of isolated products from the surface. Successful interaction analyses of the anti-Globo H antibody and MCF-7 breast cancer cells with on-chip biosynthesized Globo H-related glycans demonstrated the feasibility of the structure-switchable DNA linker-based glycan chip platform for on-chip complex glycan biosynthesis and glycan-involved applications.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"09xkv","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Pretreat thiol-modified single-stranded oligonucleotides with 1,4-dithiothreitol.
Mix single-stranded oligonucleotides (100 nmol, 1 equiv.), 1- O -allyl- D -lactose (100 nmol, 1 equiv.), and photoinitiator (10 nmol, DMPA) in 1 mL of deionized water.
Stir the mixture under UV light (365 nm) using UVP Blak-Ray® XX-15 L UV bench lamp for 2 h.

Purify the mixture by normal-phase high-performance liquid chromatography (HPLC; Gilson) with an LC-321 and OD-300 column (4.6 mm × 250 mm; PerkinElmer) using a linear gradient of acetonitrile (25-100% (v/v)) in 0.1 M triethylammonium acetate (pH 7.0).
Dissolve single-stranded i-motif DNA in printing buffer (PBS buffer, 10% (v/v) N,N -dimethylformamide; pH 4.5) to a final concentration of 70 μM.
Spot the i-motif DNA solution on gold-coated glass slide using a Microssys 5100 microarrayer (Cartesian Technologies) with a Chip Maker 2 pin.
Incubate the slide for 12 h under 75% humidity.
Treat the slide with blocking solution.
Rinse the slide twice with PBS buffer (pH 4.5) and once deionized water.
Dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.
Treat i-motif DNA-immobilized chip with 40 μL of hybridization solution (pH 9.0) containing 1 nmol lactose-oligonucleotide conjugates and agitate gently the slide for 3 h.
Wash the slide with 1X SSC solution with 0.2% (w/v) sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS), 0.1X SSC solution with 0.2% (w/v) SDS, 0.1X SSC solution, and deionized water for 1 min each.
Dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.
Prepare lactose disaccharide-immobilized slides as described above.
Divide lactose disaccharide-immobilized surface into several blocks using Gene Frame.
Drop solution of enzymes (4 mU) and nucleotide donors (10 mM) into all blocks to synthesize Globo H series from surface-immobilized lactose disaccharide in consecutive order.
Incubate each slide at 37 °C for 12 h, 24 h, 48 h, and 72 h in a humidified chamber.
Wash the slide once with washing buffer I and twice with washing buffer II and dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.
Drop PBS buffer (pH 4.5) onto the chip where enzymatic glycosylation was carried out.
Incubate at room temperature for 2 h.
Collect the solutions and desalt them using an NAP-10 column.
Evaporate the eluted products.
Analyze the products by liquid chromatography with a CarboPac PA100 column and an Ag/AgCl reference electrode using isocratic elution mode with 100 mM sodium hydroxide.
Place the Envi-Carb SPE column in a 15 mL conical tube to purify the eluted sample by solid-phase extraction chromatography.
Equilibrate the Envi-Carb column using 80% (v/v) acetonitrile in 0.1% (v/v) trifluoroacetic acid and ultrapure water.
Spin at 60 × g for 50 s.
Add a 1 mL sample to the Envi-Carb column.
Wash the column with 2 mL of ultrapure water, 2 mL of 25% (v/v) acetonitrile, 1 mL of ultrapure water, and 2 mL of 10 mM triethylammonium acetate (pH 7.0) sequentially.
Elute the final product with 2 mL of 25% (v/v) acetonitrile in 50 mM triethylammonium acetate (pH 7.0) and collect the product.
Dry the product to remove the solvent.
Dissolve the product in D 2 O and measure nuclear magnetic resonance (NMR).
Prepare the slides in which enzymatic glycosylations are sequentially completed.
Incubate the slides for 1 h with complexes of biotinylated GS-IB 4 , RCA 120 , SBA, LTL, and MAL II labeled by streptavidin-Alexa Fluor® 647 to assess the products of enzymatic reactions.
Wash the slides once with washing buffer I and twice with washing buffer II and dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.
Scan the slides using laser scanner for image acquisition.
Prepare the slides in which enzymatic glycosylations are sequentially completed under optimized reaction conditions.

Incubate the slides for 1 h with DyLight 650-conjugated anti-Gb5 monoclonal antibody, DyLight 650-conjugated anti-SSEA-4 monoclonal antibody and complexes of anti-Globo H monoclonal antibody (VK9) and anti-Gb3 monoclonal antibody labeled by goat anti-mouse IgG H&L-Alexa Fluor® 647 to determine whether there were unreacted starting glycans on the chip after each enzymatic reaction.
Wash the slides once with washing buffer I and twice with washing buffer II and dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.
Scan the slides using laser scanner for image acquisition.
Divide lactose disaccharide-immobilized surface into five different blocks using Gene Frame.
Drop a 25 μL solution of LgtC (4 mU), UDP-Gal (10 mM), Tris-HCl (100 mM; pH 7.0), and MgCl 2 (10 mM) into all five blocks.
Incubate at 37 °C for 48 h in a humidified chamber.
Wash the slide once with washing buffer I and twice with washing buffer II and dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.
Drop a 25 μL solution of LgtD (4 mU), UDP-GalNAc (10 mM), Tris-HCl (100 mM; pH 7.0), and MgCl 2 (10 mM) into four blocks of Gb3 trisaccharide-synthesized five blocks.
Incubate at 37 °C for 48 h in a humidified chamber.
Wash the slide once with washing buffer I and twice with washing buffer II and dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.
Drop a 25 μL solution of LgtD (4 mU), UDP-Gal (10 mM), Tris-HCl (100 mM; pH 7.0), and MgCl 2 (10 mM) into three of four Gb4 tetrasaccharide-synthesized blocks.
Incubate at 37 °C for 72 h in a humidified chamber.
Wash the slide once with washing buffer I and twice with washing buffer II and dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.

Drop a 25 μL solution of α1,2-FucT (4 mU), GDP-Fucose (10 mM), Tris-HCl (100 mM; pH 7.0), and MgCl 2 (10 mM) into one of three Gb5 pentasaccharide-synthesized blocks and a 25 μL solution of α2,3-SialT (4 mU), CMP-Neu5Ac (10 mM), Tris-HCl (100 mM; pH 7.5), and MgCl 2 (20 mM) into one of three Gb5 pentasaccharide-synthesized blocks.
Incubate at 37 °C for 48 h in a humidified chamber.
Wash the slide once with washing buffer I and twice with washing buffer II and dry the slide by centrifugation at 213 × g for 3 min.
Warm the following reagents at 37 °C.
Dulbecco's Modified Eagle Medium (high glucose) supplemented with 10% (v/v) heat-inactivated FBS, 100 U/mL penicillin, and 100 μg/mL streptomycin.
Mammary Epithelial Basal Medium, which contains bovine pituitary extract, hydrocortisone, human epidermal growth factor, insulin, gentamicin, and amphotericin-B.
Seed MCF-7 breast cancer cells in 75 cm² culture flask filled with DMEM culture medium and MCF-10A normal breast cells in 75 cm² culture flask filled with MEBM culture medium.
Incubate both cell lines at 37 °C in a humidified atmosphere of 5% CO 2 and 95% air and subculture them every 3 days.
Detached and centrifuge the cells.
Prepare dye-conjugated Globo H hexasaccharide
Mix Alexa Fluor® 488 hydrazide (1.75 μmol, 1 equiv.) and Globo H hexasaccharide (0.88 μmol, 0.5 equiv.) in 1 mL of 100 mM PBS buffer (pH 7.0).
Incubate the mixture at 37 °C for 6 h in a humidity chamber.
Measure liquid chromatography-mass spectrometry to confirm the reaction product.
Treat collected both cell lines with Alexa Fluor ® 488-conjugated Globo H hexasaccharide in culture medium at 37 °C for 1 h in a humidified atmosphere of 5% CO 2 and 95% air.
Centrifuge each solution and remove the supernatant solution.
Wash the cells carefully with culture medium and DPBS.
Resuspend glycan-treated and nontreated cells in DPBS.
Place these cells into the wells of a noncoated 96-well plate.
Sort by FACS.
Culture MCF-7 breast cancer and MCF-10A normal breast cells as described above.
Treat both cell lines (4 ' 10 5 cell/mL) with 4 nM calcein-AM in DPBS for 15 min.
Centrifuge each solution and remove the supernatant solution.
Wash the cells carefully with DPBS and culture medium.
Resuspend dye-treated cells in cell culture medium and apply these solutions onto the glycan chip at 37 °C for 1 h in a humidified atmosphere of 5% CO 2 and 95% air.
Wash the chip carefully once with cell culture medium and twice with DPBS.
Scan the chip using laser scanner.
Prepare calcein-AM-treated MCF-7 and MCF-10A cells as described above.
Resuspend dye-treated cells in cell culture medium.
Mix these cells in three ratios (MCF-7 cells accounted for 100%, 50%, and 10%) and apply them onto the glycan chip.
Wash the cells carefully with culture medium and DPBS.
Scan the chip using laser scanner.

Glycan chip based on structure-switchable DNA linker for on-chip biosynthesis of cancer-associated complex glycans by Hye Ryoung Heo, Kye Il Joo, Jeong Hyun Seo, Chang Sup Kim, Hyung Joon Cha published in Nature Communications on 02 Mar, 2021 https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s41467-021-21538-0
creator: Protocols Importer
